inlineServices.name: QuotaManager
inlineServices.rule: Inline only. F426 does not exist and must never be generated or injected.

crossFlowReadDependencies.purpose: Legal hold and schema-governance read before irreversible purge.
Tenant-scoped transactional event facts for correlation analytics.
Tenant-scoped review and reputation facts for correlation analytics.

freedomConfigAdaptation.description: Tenant-safe analytics policy values that can change without weakening tenant isolation, platform-only security gates, irreversible purge safeguards, or event contracts.

keys.semantic: Default alert thresholds for tenant KPI dashboards.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune dashboard sensitivity while alert evaluation remains tenant-scoped.

keys.semantic: Default metric alert rules for warehouse alerting.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune which alerts fire without changing event schemas or role gates.
Tenants may adapt cohort windows while cohort membership stays tenant-scoped.

keys.semantic: Default policy age before governance can consider data purge.

boundedRange.rationale: Retention policy may vary by tenant, but legal hold and approval-token gates remain MACHINE.

keys.semantic: Daily query quota before T187 denies execution.

boundedRange.rationale: Tenants may tune cost controls while quota remains the first gate before warehouse reads.
Tenants may choose shorter or longer export availability while encryption, audit, and masking remain mandatory.

keys.semantic: Maximum rows in a tenant training dataset export.

boundedRange.rationale: Dataset scale may adapt while PII masking and tenant scoping remain fixed.

keys.semantic: Latency threshold used by warehouse health monitoring.

boundedRange.rationale: Support teams may tune health sensitivity while the monitor remains read-only.

grammarAdaptation.description: FLOW-13 has no tenant-editable grammar surface. Tenants may tune analytics policy values and report presentation copy without changing query parsing, event schemas, or security gates.

roleScopeAdaptation.description: Tenant-admin analytics operations, tenant-user read access, platform-admin support, and platform-support diagnostics are fixed for certification. Tenant adaptation may not weaken role isolation or expose private analytics records across tenants.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Tenant analytics policies that can change while fixed warehouse ingestion, security, export, retention, and correlation invariants remain intact.

edgeCases.name: Enterprise query quota
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can raise its daily warehouse query quota for enterprise analytics users.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T173 invokes T187 quota before RLS and masking; T187 denies when maxQueriesPerDay is exceeded.

edgeCases.name: Strict export window
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can shorten export link lifetime for sensitive reporting teams.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T181 emits DataExportReady only after encryption, audit, and PII masking.

edgeCases.name: Quarterly retention analysis
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can broaden retention cohorts to a 90 day window.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T178 reads retention period policy before computing tenant-scoped cohort results.

edgeCases.name: Sensitive KPI thresholds
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A can lower KPI thresholds so alerts fire earlier.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T175 computes alertFired against FREEDOM thresholds and stores tenant-scoped snapshots.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenants cannot change without breaking portability or authorization certification.
rules: Task type range remains T169 through T188.
F426 does not exist; T187 QuotaManager is an inline service only.
T173 gate order remains T187 quota, F422 row-level security, then F423 PII masking.
F411 export encryption, F412 export audit, F422 row-level security, F423 PII masking, F424 data governance, and F425 warehouse audit are platform-only.
All cross-flow joins include tenantId in every predicate.
T186 legal-hold check runs before approval-token validation and purge.
T186 emits purge evidence with tombstone references only, never raw content or payload fields.
All analytics, cohort, retention, export, lineage, and health records remain tenant-scoped.
Store-before-enqueue ordering remains mandatory for scheduled report and retention operations.
FLOW-11, FLOW-09, and FLOW-10 are declared as co-install metadata where needed; their source is not bundled into FLOW-13 forks.

tenant-a-enterprise-analytics.description: Tenant A raises query quota and broadens retention cohort analysis for enterprise reporting.
tenant-a-enterprise-analytics.impact: Enterprise analytics users can run more warehouse queries and view longer retention cohorts while quota, RLS, masking, and tenant scope remain enforced.
tenant-a-enterprise-analytics.testable: Tenant A adaptation evidence shows the policy in tenant.config.json adaptationHistory and fork CI still passes.

tenant-a-sensitive-export-mode.description: Tenant A shortens export availability and lowers KPI thresholds for sensitive data teams.
tenant-a-sensitive-export-mode.impact: Exports expire faster and dashboards alert earlier without changing encryption, audit, masking, or query gate order.
tenant-a-sensitive-export-mode.testable: Tenant A before/after assertion shows the adapted policy in the fork package without changing MACHINE invariants.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: MACHINE elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.
elements: Task type IDs T169 through T188
PII masking before serialization or export
Approval token and legal hold checks before purge

passCriteria: Tenant A can apply a TYPE-A analytics policy change without altering MACHINE constraints.
The adapted policy is present in tenant.config.json adaptationHistory.
At least one before/after behavior assertion proves the adapted policy changes quota, export TTL, retention window, or KPI threshold behavior.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing token, 401 for cross-tenant token, and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
